response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, testing, and craftsmanship credentials, but the resume shows no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU or accelerator architecture, performance analysis of deep-learning workloads, or hardware-software co-design. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby and some embedded/robotics exposure, which falls well short of the principal-level hardware performance architecture expertise this role requires.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and community teaching/workshops
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or silicon/hardware architecture experience anywhere in the resume
No deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU workload analysis, or SIMD/MIMD background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture — a fundamental domain mismatch for a principal IC hardware role

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DL workload optimization background
